description: Trade-realistic intake flow. Branches follow actual field diagnostic sequences from Carrier, Trane, Lennox, Goodman, and Rheem troubleshooting manuals, HVAC School curriculum, and ACHR News trade publications. Questions assume the tech is on-site with basic tools (multimeter, manifold gauges, thermometer).

universal_steps_before_branch.title: What brought you to this call?

options.label: No cooling
options.subtitle: System runs but space isn't getting cold

options.label: No heating
options.subtitle: System runs but space isn't getting warm

options.label: Unusual noise
options.subtitle: New sound the system didn't make before

options.label: Weak or no airflow
options.subtitle: Air barely coming out of vents

options.label: Gas furnace + AC
options.subtitle: Furnace for heat, condenser for cool

universal_steps_before_branch.title: Thermostat status?
universal_steps_before_branch.note: Every diagnostic starts here — rule out the thermostat before touching the equipment.

options.label: Set correctly, but not sending a call
Thermostat works but system isn't responding
Running — fan and compressor both on
Trips breaker when it tries to start
Kicking on and off every few minutes

no_cooling.title: What's coming from the supply vents?

options.label: Room temp air — blower running but no cooling
Slightly cool but not reaching setpoint
Good cold air, then it stops

no_cooling.title: Visual inspection — select all that apply:

options.label: Ice on suction line or evaporator coil
Condenser coil is dirty / blocked with debris

no_cooling.title: If you've taken readings, select what you found:
no_cooling.note: Optional — only if you have gauges hooked up or have taken temp readings.

no_heating.title: What type of heating is this system using?
no_heating.note: This determines the entire diagnostic path — gas and electric have completely different failure modes.

no_heating.title: What happens when you call for heat?

options.label: Blower runs but air isn't warm
Inducer kicks on but burners never light
Burners light then shut off (seconds to minutes)
Heat pump running but blowing cold air

no_heating.title: At the unit — select all that apply:

options.label: Control board LED flashing an error code
Smell of gas near the furnace
Igniter not glowing or gas valve not opening
Flame sensor rod looks dirty or corroded
High limit or rollout switch tripped
Everything — indoor and outdoor both dead

wont_start.title: Power checks — select all that apply:

options.label: Fuse blown or disconnect switch is off
No 24V at thermostat terminals or transformer issue
Control board has no LED / no power indicator
Float switch tripped or blower door open

wont_start.title: Did anything happen before it stopped?

short_cycling.title: How long does it run before shutting off?
What's cycling — indoor, outdoor, or both?

options.label: Outdoor unit cycles, indoor keeps running
Indoor unit cycles, outdoor keeps running
High head pressure or dirty condenser coil
Furnace: limit tripping, flame going out, or pressure switch cycling
System may be oversized for the space

unusual_noise.title: Where is the noise coming from?

options.label: Outdoor unit — compressor or fan area

unusual_noise.title: Best description of the sound?
unusual_noise.note: Sound type maps directly to likely component failure.

options.label: Banging or slamming
options.subtitle: Often loose or broken internal part

options.label: Repeated clicking
options.subtitle: Relay, contactor, or ignition trying to start

options.label: High-pitched screaming
options.subtitle: SHUT IT DOWN — compressor high pressure

options.label: Boom or pop on ignition
options.subtitle: Delayed ignition — gas buildup before light-off

options.label: Only when system is under heavy load
Running but weak — air barely coming out
Sounds normal but vents have low flow
Running but making a high-pitched sound
Filter is clogged or heavily restricted
Ductwork disconnected, collapsed, or registers closed/blocked
Blower motor hot, capacitor weak, or ECM fault

universal_steps_after_branch.title: Anything else the agent should know?

options.label: Past few days to a week
Seasonal — happens when it gets hot/cold
Comes and goes — no clear pattern

universal_steps_after_branch.placeholder: Recent work done, readings you've taken, anything unusual about the install, customer complaints, conditions at the site...

headline.description: Plain English fault name in trade language.
headline.example: Dirty flame sensor causing ignition lockout

severity.note: Critical = safety hazard (gas leak, cracked heat exchanger, CO risk). High = system down. Medium = degraded performance. Low = minor / maintenance item.

system_area.description: Which part of the system is affected.

what_was_detected.description: 2-4 sentence summary of what the symptom combination points to. Trade language.
what_was_detected.example: Inducer runs, igniter glows, gas valve opens, but flame drops out within 4 seconds and system locks out after 3 attempts. Flame sensor is likely dirty or failed — it's not proving flame to the control board.

likely_causes.description: Ranked by probability based on symptom combination. Most likely first.
example: Dirty flame sensor — most common cause of ignition lockout

based_on_your_answers.description: Summary of key inputs for verification.
based_on_your_answers.example: System: gas furnace + AC, 10–15 years old · Complaint: no heating · Behavior: burners light then flame goes out · Visual: flame sensor dirty, LED flashing error · Timeline: started today

safety_flags.description: Any safety concerns identified from the intake. Empty array if none.
example: Rollout switch tripped — possible cracked heat exchanger. Recommend CO test before resetting.

confidence.note: Low = suggest tech provide more info or hook up instruments.

prompt_assembly.system_prompt: You are an HVAC diagnostic agent used by field technicians. You receive structured intake data from a tech who is on-site at the equipment. Your job: identify the most likely fault based on the combination of answers provided. Rules: (1) Speak in trade language — suction pressure, superheat, flame rectification, not homeowner terms. (2) Never recommend a specific fix or part to buy. Identify the fault — the tech decides the repair. (3) If the inputs suggest a safety hazard (gas leak, CO risk, cracked heat exchanger, high-pressure screaming), flag it as critical severity and include a safety_flags warning. (4) If inputs are insufficient for a confident diagnosis, say so — set confidence to low and tell the tech what additional readings or checks would narrow it down. (5) Return ONLY valid JSON matching the output_template. No markdown, no commentary, no preamble.
